# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/products_controller.rb
class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.all
    @products = filter_products(@products, search_params)
    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  def search
    @products = Product.all
    @products = filter_products(@products, search_params)
    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render :index }
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  private

  def search_params
    return {} unless params[:search].present?

    params.require(:search).permit(
      :name,
      :category,
      :category_id,
      :min_price,
      :max_price,
      :sort_by,
      :sort_direction,
      :in_stock,
      :brand,
      tags: []
    )
  end

  def filter_products(scope, filters)
    scope = filter_by_name(scope, filters[:name])
    scope = filter_by_category(scope, filters[:category], filters[:category_id])
    scope = filter_by_price_range(scope, filters[:min_price], filters[:max_price])
    scope = filter_by_stock(scope, filters[:in_stock])
    scope = filter_by_brand(scope, filters[:brand])
    scope = filter_by_tags(scope, filters[:tags])
    scope = apply_sorting(scope, filters[:sort_by], filters[:sort_direction])
    scope
  end

  def filter_by_name(scope, name)
    return scope if name.blank?

    # SECURE: Using parameterized query instead of string interpolation
    # NEVER do this: scope.where("name LIKE '%#{name}%'") -- SQL INJECTION!
    scope.where("products.name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(name)}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope, category_name, category_id)
    if category_id.present?
      # SECURE: Using hash conditions
      scope.where(category_id: category_id)
    elsif category_name.present?
      # SECURE: Using parameterized join query
      scope.joins(:category).where("categories.name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(category_name)}%")
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope, min_price, max_price)
    # SECURE: Using parameterized queries for range filtering
    if min_price.present? && max_price.present?
      scope.where(price: BigDecimal(min_price.to_s)..BigDecimal(max_price.to_s))
    elsif min_price.present?
      scope.where("products.price >= ?", BigDecimal(min_price.to_s))
    elsif max_price.present?
      scope.where("products.price <= ?", BigDecimal(max_price.to_s))
    else
      scope
    end
  rescue ArgumentError
    # Handle invalid price values gracefully
    scope
  end

  def filter_by_stock(scope, in_stock)
    return scope if in_stock.blank?

    if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(in_stock)
      scope.where("products.stock_quantity > ?", 0)
    else
      scope.where(stock_quantity: 0)
    end
  end

  def filter_by_brand(scope, brand)
    return scope if brand.blank?

    # SECURE: Using hash conditions
    scope.where(brand: brand)
  end

  def filter_by_tags(scope, tags)
    return scope if tags.blank?

    # SECURE: Using parameterized IN clause
    scope.joins(:tags).where(tags: { name: tags }).distinct
  end

  def apply_sorting(scope, sort_by, sort_direction)
    # SECURE: Whitelist allowed sort columns to prevent SQL injection
    allowed_sort_columns = %w[name price created_at updated_at stock_quantity]
    allowed_directions = %w[asc desc]

    sort_column = allowed_sort_columns.include?(sort_by) ? sort_by : "created_at"
    direction = allowed_directions.include?(sort_direction&.downcase) ? sort_direction.downcase : "desc"

    scope.order("products.#{sort_column} #{direction}")
  end

  # Helper to sanitize LIKE wildcards in user input
  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(string)
  end
end
